headline: Selling Broad, Energy Alone in the Green as US-Iran Conflict Rattles Friday Close
body_html:
Friday's session delivered a punishing finish to the trading week, with decliners outnumbering advancers by more than two to one — 348 stocks lower against just 150 higher across the 501-name universe. Every major sector finished in the red save Energy, which edged up 0.78%, lifted by rising oil prices as US-Iran hostilities entered a seventh consecutive night of strikes and threats to Red Sea shipping lanes intensified. Homebuilders bore the steepest losses, off 3.05%, followed by Consumer, Industrials, and Healthcare, each shedding roughly 1.25%.

The session's sharpest single-stock damage fell on Healthcare Devices and Electronic Design Automation software. Intuitive Surgical plunged 14.15%, the worst decline in the universe, while Cadence Design and Synopsys dropped 9.47% and 7.85% respectively — a notable double blow to the EDA space. STAAR Surgical shed 8.76%, compounding the weakness in medical devices. On the positive side, Sonos surged 5.89% to lead all gainers, followed by Seagate Technology at 5.66% and Ralliant at 4.86%.

Heading into the new week, the geopolitical backdrop remains the dominant macro variable. Gold posted its largest weekly decline since early June amid inflation and rate-hike concerns, while oil's bid reflects sustained uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz. Traders will be watching whether diplomatic signals emerge from Washington or Tehran, and how earnings reporters navigate guidance in a conflict-clouded demand environment.

briefs.headline: Seventh Consecutive Night of US Strikes on Iran Keeps Markets on Edge
briefs.body: American forces completed their latest round of strikes on Iran, marking seven straight nights of military action. The sustained campaign, triggered in part by Iranian attacks that killed two US service members in Jordan, has kept risk sentiment fragile and elevated demand for energy assets heading into the new week.

briefs.headline: Red Sea Shipping Corridor Faces Fresh Disruption Threat
briefs.body: US and Iranian forces have both targeted infrastructure as attacks on strait shipping routes intensified through the weekend. The threat of a broader closure of the waterway raises supply-chain concerns across energy, goods, and freight markets — a potential headwind for industrials and consumer-facing sectors already under pressure.

briefs.headline: Oil Firms on Conflict Premium; Gold Suffers Its Worst Weekly Skid in Six Weeks
briefs.body: Crude settled higher Friday as renewed US-Iran hostilities stoked supply-risk fears, giving Energy its lone sectoral gain on the day. Gold moved in the opposite direction, logging its steepest weekly drop since early June as inflation concerns and the prospect of higher interest rates weighed on the non-yielding metal.

briefs.headline: EDA Software Stocks Hit Hard in Back-to-Back Declines
briefs.body: Cadence Design fell 9.47% and Synopsys slid 7.85% on Friday, making the Electronic Design Automation space one of the session's most notable trouble spots. The paired selloff dragged on the broader Software sector, which finished down nearly 1%, and raises questions about near-term order visibility in chip-design tooling.

briefs.headline: Intuitive Surgical Leads Healthcare Devices Lower with Sharp Double-Digit Drop
briefs.body: Intuitive Surgical tumbled 14.15% on Friday — the largest single-session decline in the tracked universe — pulling the Healthcare sector down 1.24% overall. STAAR Surgical added to the pain with an 8.76% retreat, leaving medical-device names as a key area of investor concern entering the earnings-heavy stretch ahead.

briefs.headline: Boeing Holds Firm on Long-Range Jet Demand Despite Iran War Uncertainty
briefs.body: The planemaker left its 20-year commercial-jet demand forecast unchanged, signaling confidence that the Iran conflict will not materially alter long-run aviation growth trajectories. The stance offers a measure of reassurance for aerospace supply chains, though near-term travel-demand data and airline guidance this week will be closely scrutinized.
